sentence_reviews.text: Examples, populations, sampling schemes, train/validation/test splits, and provenance determine what a model can learn and how it is measured.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: Chapter 1.5 'Data', preprocessing and missing data, overfitting/generalization, and regularizer selection using validation sets / cross-validation (lines 7-20 and 123-130); AIMA contents.html: developing ML systems, data collection/assessment/management, model selection and training (lines 532-538).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support data, preprocessing, missingness, validation, cross-validation, and data management as central to learning and evaluation. Provenance is not directly named in the inspected passages.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Datasets, sampling & splits is to connect Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design to downstream capabilities including Multimodal, simulated & streaming data, Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: probability chapters, statistics chapter, data section, supervised and unsupervised learning sections (lines 26-147 and 2-15); AIMA contents.html: supervised learning, data collection/assessment/management, and unsupervised learning entries (lines 495-538 and 584-590).
sentence_reviews.notes: The probability/statistics and supervised/unsupervised portions are supported, but multimodal, simulated, and streaming data plus experimental design are not directly established in the inspected locations.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: Chapter 1.3 'Unsupervised learning' and Chapter 21 clustering (lines 9-13 and 648-670); AIMA contents.html: Chapter 21.7.1 'Unsupervised learning' (lines 584-590).

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: preprocessing discrete/text data, missing data, probability, statistics, cross-validation, and caveats (lines 18-24 and 26-147); AIMA contents.html: fairness and bias plus data collection/assessment/management (lines 745-749 and 532-538).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support preprocessing, missing data, bias as an AI concern, probability, and statistics. The invariance and experimental-design clauses are not directly supported by the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Datasets, sampling & splits rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.

sentence_reviews.text: Datasets, sampling & splits rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Statistics & experimental design.

sentence_reviews.text: Datasets, sampling & splits rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Databases & information retrieval.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html: developing ML systems, especially data collection, assessment, and management (lines 532-538), and nearest-neighbor / locality-sensitive hashing entries (lines 518-524).
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence supports data management and retrieval-like methods as relevant, but not as a required formal dependency for datasets/splits.

edge_reviews.notes: Sampling and train/test population reasoning depend on probability concepts such as random variables, distributions, and uncertainty.
Statistics is directly tied to estimation, sampling distributions, validation, and cross-validation, so the required/core edge is defensible.
Data management is relevant to datasets, but the inspected evidence does not make databases/IR a conceptual prerequisite. The 'common' strength is more defensible than 'core'.
